what_it_does: Acceptance test for the activation driver decision layer. It exercises background_tick idle-gate firing, sense_tick raw-event shaping and dispatch, RollupDriver cursor-based consolidation, and mode auto-detection feeding the existing off/suggest/auto toggle, using synthetic signals and a recording stub over the activation entry points.
observations: The file is organized into labeled sections: drift checks against runtime/AGENTS.md, shared activity reader, H1 background driver, H2 sense driver, H3 rollup driver, I1 mode auto-detector, and an optional live-path smoke.
A _Recorder stub intercepts suite.fire_activation and suite.consolidate_rollup so driver decisions can be verified deterministically without invoking a live model or GPU.
Drift assertions verify that OPERATOR_ACTIVITY_KINDS, the mode_detection_rules registry, the H drivers, the I detector, and the always-on caller posture are documented in runtime/AGENTS.md.
activity_signal is tested as a pure read that reports mode, idle_seconds, last_activity, inbox, and recent_kinds, and excludes the system's own background activity kinds.
background_tick tests cover no-fire while the operator is active, fire when idle past threshold in a background-allocating mode, and refusal when the mode does not allocate background.

sense_tick tests cover shaping a raw dict into a sense_event, preserving original fields, honoring an explicit summary, failing loud on non-dict input, and refusing when the mode does not allocate sense.
RollupDriver tests cover no-op on empty intervals, consolidation of only new waves since the held cursor, cursor advancement, and no re-consolidation of already-processed waves.

Mode detection tests cover registered-mode validation for every discovered rule, deterministic background candidate on long idle, and propose_mode behavior under suggest, auto, and off toggle settings.
The optional live-path block only asserts if the resident model is reachable; otherwise it prints a skip message.
standouts: The _Recorder stub decouples driver-decision verification from live cast execution, making the test deterministic and GPU-independent.
RollupDriver cursor semantics are explicitly proven: only waves after the held cursor are consolidated, and the cursor advances without re-processing old waves.
The detector is proven to feed the existing off/suggest/auto toggle rather than calling set_mode directly.
conventions: Uses a global PASS counter and a check(label, cond) helper for all assertions.
Creates a fresh Suite instance per scenario via fresh_suite to isolate state.
Injects synthetic operator-activity events with controlled ISO timestamps and an explicit now_epoch to make idle measurements deterministic.
The recording stub returns ActivationResult-shaped objects so downstream shape checks pass.
Drift assertions mirror the pattern of coupling runtime behavior to AGENTS.md documentation.
concerns: The global PASS counter and check() helper mutate shared state and are not thread-safe.
The _Recorder stub hardcodes ActivationResult field values that could drift from the real production shape.
The optional live-path block catches a broad Exception and only prints, so CI may silently skip real-path coverage.
Several assertions rely on exact substring wording in reason strings, making them brittle to message changes.
The test monkey-patches suite.fire_activation and suite.consolidate_rollup directly rather than using a formal fixture or dependency injection.
notes: Acceptance harness that proves the activation driver decision layer with synthetic signals and stubs, plus drift checks and an optional live-path smoke.
questions: What is the exact production shape of act.ActivationResult, and does the stub's return value stay aligned with it?
How is mode_detection_rule_registry.ordered() implemented, and where are the rule files located?
What are the configured threshold values for idle_seconds in background_tick and in the detection rules?
What does the always-on caller documentation 'needs-tim (no daemon stood up)' mean operationally?
Are the referenced activation_contexts_acceptance tests the ones that prove the live cast path?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it proves the net-new decision layer over already-built activation entry points using synthetic signals and a recording stub, with drift checks and an optional live path.
apparent_themes: Deterministic verification of decision logic independent of GPU or live model.
Drift-aware documentation that must reflect runtime registries and drivers.
Gate, intake, and cursor semantics with legible no-fire reasons.
Delegation to existing entry points rather than duplicating them.
Mode auto-detection as a feed to an existing toggle, not a direct mode override.
intention_signals: The file is trying to isolate and validate the new driver layer's policy decisions—when to fire background, how to intake sense events, how to hold a rollup cursor, and how to propose modes—while reusing and not re-proving the underlying activation entry points.
novelty: It uses a recording stub to prove driver-layer decisions without invoking the live 4B cast, and it includes explicit drift assertions coupling code behavior to AGENTS.md documentation.
contribution: A deterministic acceptance test that defines expected driver semantics for background gating, sense event shaping, rollup cursor advancement, and mode-proposal toggle feeding, plus a reusable _Recorder pattern for stubbing activation entry points.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for the activation driver decision layer covering background_tick idle gate, sense_tick event intake, RollupDriver cursor consolidation, and mode auto-detector toggle feeding using synthetic signals and a recording stub.
intention_for_embedding: To prove the net-new driver decisions deterministically without GPU dependency while ensuring drift documentation reflects the new registries and drivers.

symbols.does: Global assertion helper that increments PASS on success.
symbols.description: Takes a label and a condition, asserts the condition with a prefixed failure message, increments the global PASS counter, and prints an ok line. It is the single assertion primitive used throughout the acceptance test.

symbols.does: Factory that returns an isolated Suite with a temporary FsStore and discovered nodes.
symbols.description: Creates a temporary directory, initializes an FsStore, discovers nodes from the nodes directory, and returns a fresh Suite instance so each test scenario starts from clean state.

symbols.does: Recording stub over fire_activation and consolidate_rollup for deterministic driver testing.
symbols.description: Class that captures calls to the activation entry points so the test can verify driver decisions without invoking the live model cast. It returns ActivationResult-shaped objects.

symbols.does: Initializes empty call logs for fired activations and rollups.
symbols.description: Sets up two lists, fired and rollups, used to record every invocation of the stubbed entry points.

symbols.does: Records a fire_activation call and returns a stub ActivationResult.
symbols.description: Appends the context, mode, and sense_event to fired, then returns a canned ActivationResult with context, mode, trigger, reply flag, fired_roles, and routed destination so downstream shape checks pass.

symbols.does: Records a consolidate_rollup call and returns a stub rollup result.
symbols.description: Appends the since, mode, and gc arguments to rollups, then returns a canned ActivationResult whose rollup field reports one wave and two role runs.

symbols.does: Attaches a _Recorder instance to a Suite's entry-point methods.
symbols.description: Creates a _Recorder and assigns it to suite.fire_activation and suite.consolidate_rollup, returning the recorder so tests can inspect captured calls.

symbols.does: Helper that builds a fresh suite at a given mode and toggle with a controlled stale activity event.
symbols.description: Creates a fresh Suite, sets the mode and RHM config toggle, then appends a single old chat event as the last event so the idle signal is deterministically large for propose_mode tests.
